# Source Card: Disease progression and clinical outcomes in telomere biology disorders

## Summary
According to PubMed, this peer-reviewed Blood (2022) prospective cohort of 231 dyskeratosis congenita/telomere biology disorder (DC/TBD) individuals from the NCI Inherited Bone Marrow Failure Syndrome Study (NCT00027274) characterizes how germline inheritance mode (autosomal dominant vs autosomal recessive/X-linked vs TINF2) drives phenotype and long-term survival. It establishes cohort-level survival, transplant, bone-marrow-failure, cancer, and pulmonary-fibrosis outcomes stratified by inheritance and age. For the pediatric CDS module it supplies inheritance-mode-based risk-stratification evidence and age-at-diagnosis distributions — NOT laboratory reference intervals or CBC assay thresholds.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)

- (ev_002) 42% deceased; median OS 52.8 y (95% CI 45.5-57.6) — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:fdd838f3f1f871d94ddd8ce700a058f128fd131db3f59ca533aaa62cf9773aae]"
- (ev_003) Transplant-free median survival 45.3 y (95% CI 37.4-52.1); AD better than AR/XLR/TINF2 (P<.01) — locator: Abstract — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:7320e4ce181d6cfc8671c635c94f978d07501115df1074a37fdcd80635c9b542]"
- (ev_004) Severe BMF in 111 (48.1%); HCT in 70 (30.3%) at median age 15.4 y — locator: Results / Table 3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:59749cbfa6d016fea87e240058eaa996526e57a4e8cf1021a90e65fa9499654d]"
- (ev_005) AR/XLR cancer hazard ratio 8 (95% CI 1.93-33.51) vs AD — locator: Results — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:54a5c447f06417d22cbb936513ba403fde23e8ad2c522e104f659145d9dfa704]"
- (ev_006) Median age at diagnosis: AD 36.1 y, AR/XLR 11.3 y, TINF2 8.1 y — locator: Results / Table 1 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:170e07ccbabf249f3f14a7d346354d9a523f4995fca0377915856c1e2bc27135]"
- (ev_007) Pulmonary fibrosis 19.6% in AD adults vs 9.5% AR/XLR vs 8% TINF2 — locator: Results / Table 3 — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:a7dc5cd4bfab663de4827eac17198f1cae3882f4c8f01c4f3aa61f33b4d4ed39]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: DC/TBD patients across the lifespan; AR/XLR and TINF2 subtypes present predominantly in childhood (median diagnosis 11.3 y and 8.1 y), whereas AD presents predominantly in adulthood (median 36.1 y). Pulmonary fibrosis is an adult-predominant phenotype in this cohort.
- Assay/method: None of the reported numbers are laboratory assay results — they are clinical-outcome, survival, and demographic statistics; therefore none are analyzer/method-dependent and none constitute a CBC reference interval.
- Lifecycle: Published 2022-03 (Blood 139(12):1807-1819); primary research study, not a guideline — no guideline_version, effective/retire, or supersedes semantics apply. Trial registration NCT00027274.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Relevant to risk stratification, not to a CBC numeric decision threshold: the source supports incorporating germline inheritance mode (AD vs AR/XLR vs TINF2) into DC/TBD risk stratification, and flags severe bone marrow failure and cancer as high-burden events in AR/XLR/TINF2 pediatric-onset disease. Any CDS use is an implementation_proposal for the converter, not an executable rule — this card asserts only the source-supported facts, not diagnostic/treatment/transplant directives.

## Limitations & conflicts
- This is an epidemiological outcomes paper, NOT a reference-interval / assay-threshold source: no CBC (hemoglobin, platelet, ANC) numeric cutoffs are locatable here, so none are asserted. Threshold X (any CBC decision cutoff) not locatable in source 34852175 — recorded as a GAP.
- Median survival and prevalence figures are cohort-level estimates from a single NCI referral registry (possible ascertainment bias toward severe disease); they must not be treated as individual pediatric decision thresholds.
- No conflict with other sources recorded. Inheritance-mode survival differences (AD superior; AR/XLR and TINF2 similar and worse) are preserved as reported and not to be averaged against other cohorts.
